lessons.title: Types of Resolutions
lessons.description: The Companies Act 2014 recognises four types of resolution: ordinary resolutions passed by simple majority; special resolutions requiring seventy-five per cent of votes cast; unanimous written resolutions; and majority written resolutions. The type required depends on the nature of the decision — and special resolutions must be filed with the Companies Registration Office within fifteen days of being passed under Section 198.

lessons.title: General Meetings — AGMs and EGMs
lessons.description: This is a highly practical area that is regularly tested in FE-1 examinations. There are specific numbers, thresholds, and time limits that you will need to memorise, so pay close attention.

lessons.title: Director Meetings and Board Governance
lessons.description: Directors exercise their powers collectively at board meetings, or, where the constitution permits, through written resolutions of the board.
